problem: Calculate $\sqrt{30p} \cdot \sqrt{5p} \cdot \sqrt{6p}$ . Express your answer in simplest radical form in terms of $p$.

classified_questions.question: What is the relationship between multiplication and multiplication of radicals?
classified_questions.label: r

classified_questions.question: How can we simplify the expression $\sqrt{30p} \cdot \sqrt{5p} \cdot \sqrt{6p}$ by using the properties of radicals?
classified_questions.label: r

classified_questions.question: Can we combine the square roots under the radical sign? If so, how?
classified_questions.label: r

classified_questions.question: How do we simplify the expression when there are different variables involved?
classified_questions.label: r

classified_questions.question: What is the product of $\sqrt{30p}$, $\sqrt{5p}$, and $\sqrt{6p}$?
classified_questions.label: b

classified_questions.question: Can we simplify the resulting radical expression further? If yes, how? If not, why not?
classified_questions.label: b

classified_questions.question: What is the simplest radical form of the expression in terms of $p$?
classified_questions.label: b

classified_questions.question: How can we verify the correctness of our simplification by multiplying back the simplified expression?
classified_questions.label: b
